task_completion.description: Set the location to London, UK and the time to August 31, 1888, at 03:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium's bottom information bar or the Date/Time and Location windows should display the date '1888-08-31', the time '03:00' (or very close to it), and the location 'London' or coordinates near 51.5° N, 0.1° W.

task_completion.description: Configure the view to include the landscape, atmosphere, cardinal points, and azimuthal grid.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium sky view must show the ground (landscape), sky glow/haze (atmosphere), N/S/E/W compass letters (cardinal points), and a web of concentric circles and radiating lines (azimuthal grid). The corresponding icons on the bottom toolbar should be highlighted.

task_completion.description: Center the view on the Moon and capture a screenshot saved to ~/Pictures/stellarium/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Moon should be exactly in the center of the Stellarium view, with its phase and position relative to the horizon clearly visible. There should be evidence of a screenshot being taken or saved to the '~/Pictures/stellarium/' directory (e.g., an on-screen notification, terminal command, or file manager view).

task_completion.description: Record the date, time, target, Moon's phase, and cardinal direction in ~/Desktop/whitechapel_moon.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of 'whitechapel_moon.txt' on the Desktop. The text must include the date (Aug 31, 1888), time (03:00 UTC), target (Moon), a description of its phase, and its cardinal direction.

task_completion.description: Save all program settings, including the date and time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Configuration window (Main tab) should be visible with the 'Save settings' button clicked, or a notification indicating that settings have been saved.

integrity.description: The agent actually interacted with the Stellarium GUI to configure the scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Stellarium application open with various menus (Location, Date/Time, View, Configuration) being actively used, rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The screenshot of the Moon is genuinely from the Stellarium simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot must match the Stellarium interface and rendering style, including the requested azimuthal grid and cardinal points, not an image pulled from a web browser.

integrity.description: The notes in the text file reflect the actual simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The phase and cardinal direction described in 'whitechapel_moon.txt' should logically match what is visually depicted in the Stellarium sky view at the specified time.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Task requires setting Stellarium to London, UK (51.5 N, 0.1 W) on 1888-08-31 at 03:00:00 UTC. Required view flags: atmosphere, landscape, cardinal points, azimuthal grid. Notes must be saved to ~/Desktop/whitechapel_moon.txt and screenshots to ~/Pictures/stellarium/.
